claim: Deep agents are tool-calling loops with planning (to-do list), filesystem (context offload), sub-agents (isolated tasks), and detailed system prompts
actionable: Use deep agent architecture for complex workflows; add planning, file persistence, and task delegation as baked-in primitives
source_title: Rewriting Deep Agents on top of LangChain 1.0

claim: Middleware is a stackable abstraction that extends agent state schema, adds tools, and modifies system prompts without rewriting the core agent loop
actionable: Build agents using middleware pattern; stack planning, filesystem, and sub-agent middleware independently for modular composition
source_title: Rewriting Deep Agents on top of LangChain 1.0

claim: Sub-agents with isolated context windows save tokens by returning summaries to main agent instead of passing raw intermediate tool calls
actionable: Spawn sub-agents for narrow tasks; have them return concise summaries to main agent to reduce context bloat
source_title: Rewriting Deep Agents on top of LangChain 1.0

claim: Domain-specific sub-agents can use custom prompts and tool subsets; general-purpose sub-agents isolate context to prevent interference
actionable: Create two types of sub-agents: general-purpose for context isolation and domain-specific for focused tasks with specialized tools
source_title: Rewriting Deep Agents on top of LangChain 1.0

claim: Summarization middleware automatically compacts conversation history when context exceeds token threshold, keeping recent context available
actionable: Add auto-summarization to prevent context window overflow; balance history retention with context efficiency
source_title: Rewriting Deep Agents on top of LangChain 1.0

claim: Human-in-the-loop middleware can interrupt sensitive tool calls and allow humans to approve, edit, or reject actions before execution
actionable: Use human-in-the-loop for mission-critical tools; configure approval, editing, and feedback options for each tool
source_title: Rewriting Deep Agents on top of LangChain 1.0
